candidates.stmt: Since 2021, I’ve been proud to call this area home. You may know me from working in a local coffee shop, where I’m on the floor every day having real conversations with our community. As a father, I’m driven by the future we’re building for our children. I want them to grow up in a Besses that is safe, vibrant, and united. From running local businesses to managing youth arts programmes and volunteering to tackle food waste, I’ve seen that we are at our best when we come together. I’m here to listen and deliver.

I am Martyn West and I am your Bury Independent candidate for Besses ward in Bury Council’s local elections due to take place on Thursday 2nd May 2024.

Thank you all for your support last year when I came second to the incumbent representative. I hope you want change, and I am the person best placed to bring that about with Bury Independents.

We are a group of local people who have lost confidence in politicians especially those from national parties. We believe Bury residents deserve better with representatives who will put the residents first, not the policies of national political parties.

The local election on 2nd May is your chance to have your say. You decide who represents your interests and how your taxpayer money is spent in Bury. You are unlikely to have another chance until 2026.

6000 voters in Besses Ward do not vote. This means that 2000 voters who do, have a say. No Vote = No Say.

I am a sales assistant in a local garden centre. I am standing for the Green Party as I believe they want to bring much needed change and hope to our communities.
 
My priority for Bury East is to help people in our community who are struggling with the cost of living and lack of opportunities. As a young person working full time on minimum wage I know how difficult it is to better yourself due to a lack of opportunities and also how hard it is for everyone to survive in this current political climate which feels like the country is run on greed and wealth.

As a neurodivergent adult, I am passionate about creating a better environment for neurodivergent people of all ages, and I am determined to bring more support to people in Bury.

In the past I have volunteered for Bury Hospice to help bring in vital funds for such an important facility in our town.

I have been a youth worker for eight years and I am a mum. I love living in Bury and I want to give back to my community.

I am excited to be running as a candidate in Bury West. I am just a normal person who wants to make a positive difference. I offer a fresh perspective and hope in making politics less volatile.

I want to be a councillor who is visible and accessible. I want to work in partnership with local business, schools, community organisations and with you the residents. I will listen to your views, help and represent this constituency effectively at Bury Council.

I've lived in Bury since I was born, at Fairfield Hospital in 1999, and I went to school first at Holcombe Brook Primary School, then Woodhey High School and college at Holy Cross in Bury. Whilst many of my friends chose to go to university to study further, I chose to apply for an apprenticeship, and have been lucky to work for two Top 50 law firms whilst I worked and studied for my qualification.

This means I've been able to stay in the area my whole life, and I am very happy to call it home. I'm proud of where we live, and have for years enjoyed walking on the Kirklees Trail and in Burrs Country Park. There is nothing more rewarding than looking after our area and I have been pleased to do many litterpicks and work with local community groups to help. By going one step further and standing as a candidate, I am hoping that I will now have the chance to give more back to Bury, to Elton and to you.

🗳️Five reasons to vote for me on 7 May🗳️

✅ As a candidate I’ve worked hard week in week out for the Elton community. I don’t just turn up at election time. I want the opportunity to step this up if I’m lucky enough to get elected in May and continue getting things done for residents and businesses. I will always show up, respond, and try my best to get a positive result for you.

✅ I’ve lived in Bury for over a decade and care about making the town and Elton a better place. This is my home and I’m invested in delivering positive change. I’ve already been cracking on with the job: getting roads and streets fixed; and working with our brilliant community groups like Keep Bury Clean, Brandlesholme Community Centre and the local neighbourhood police team to improve the are and make it safer.

✅ You deserve better than an invisible councillor and a fresh start with someone who actually wants to do the job and doesn’t take residents for granted. The current Reform councillor has let residents down and is now whining that he’s been found out for taking tax payers for a free ride with less than one recorded council casework email per month.

✅ I am on the side of working people, families and the most vulnerable. I will fight for you in the Council Chamber and work with our local MP, Mayor and Government to improve local family and children services, and get more investment for better Elton community facilities.

✅ Labour is the only party with a fully costed plan to deliver for Bury. £12 million to fix our roads, regenerations projects being delivered across the borough, and more funding to deliver our key frontline services.
Reform couldn’t be bothered to present a budget or costed plans in February, and they still haven’t. They just expect you to trust them with no detail, but the truth is they’ve been a disaster for the council areas they took over last year.

As a resident I don’t want to risk Bury’s future with Reform, and hope residents will back me as a strong voice

The Elton Reservoir is our largest area of water in Bury and of great beauty for our plants and other wild life. We need to challenge our council to stop the building of more houses on the green belt.

Our roads aren't fit for purpose, not just the potholes, but the lack of cycle ways and discouraging fly-tipping: we need to challenge our council on this area that needs developing.

Parents of children with additional leaning and communication needs feel let down by the council for not providing appropriate and effective support, both in the classroom and in the community. I know as I was a headteacher in a special school for over 30 years.

Our council does little for our teenagers in the community, with few options to thrive in designated centres. I give music sessions in our local care homes and hospitals voluntarily and have become aware how little activities exist in the community for our ageing population.

A review of our council's highest paid managers is required, as Bury's finances are in a mess, even though council taxes are at their highest ever!

I am your Bury Independent candidate for Elton ward in Bury Council’s local elections due to take place on Thursday 2nd May 2024.

I am standing as the Bury Independent candidate for Elton ward in the May elections because I feel that the needs of local people will be better served by a locally based group rather than by the main national political parties. Our aim is to get the best deal for the people of Elton and Bury generally and, above all, to listen to the real views of local people.

I am standing as a councillor for Elton ward because:

Bury has been failed by the two-party system with party issues often coming before local ones.

I believe in people before politics, listening first then acting.

Local people’s views should carry most weight in major decisions.

We need to take positive action on repairing our potholes and making our roads fit for purpose.

We need to stamp out fly tipping, a horrible blot on our landscape.

We need to look again at large building developments that have a huge impact on our environment.

We need to ensure that we have the infrastructure in place to support any future developments.

I won’t make any lavish promises as to what I can do if I get elected. The one promise I can make, and keep, is to do my absolute best for the people of Elton

I have lived in Prestwich for over 20 years. Over this time I have brought up a family and worked nearby as a secondary school teacher. I have been delighted with the recent growth of the Green Party which shares my values of protecting the environment and sustainability.

As a resident in the ward I've been following the proposal for the housing development in Simister and have been engaging with other residents on this issue. If elected, I will continue to fight to protect green belt land that can be enjoyed for future generations to come.

I am your Bury Independent candidate for Moorside ward in Bury Council’s local elections due to take place on Thursday 2nd May 2024. I’d be really pleased if I could count on your vote this year, so that Bury Independents have a chance to show you how a local-only party can make a difference for you. There are no elections until 2026 after this one, so please consider lending me your vote on the 2nd May.

I would like to introduce myself as a Conservative Candidate for the Holyrood Ward in the upcoming local elections.

I am proud to call Bury my home. 

I never thought I would come to rely on the help of others but when I arrived in the UK, I found myself in a situation where I desperately needed to be able to talk to someone.

When I arrived in the UK in 2008, I couldn't speak any English, but people were so kind and welcoming. I cried and cried tears of happiness because, in North Korea, I didn't feel welcome, but in the UK, people made me feel at home.

Now that the UK is my home, I am not just going to be the recipients of other people's kindness, I am also going to support others going through difficult situations.

I know last year had been one of the most difficult and challenging moments in our history but the light will always shine in the darkness.

Last year I supported local care homes and they said that they really applaud the refugees; coming to another country as an escapee and supporting another country's people with donations.

These words gave me courage and helped me support those many people who need us.

Winston Churchill said that "Courage is what it takes to stand up and speak, Courage is also what it takes to sit down and listen"

The reason I want to stand up as Local councillor is that I think residents need to have a lot of patience, be dedicated, passionate about becoming involved with the community and just generally interested in the local democracy and trying to make the community in which you live in much better. These are the qualities I possess.

If elected, I will be listening to our residents, our neighbours and I will protect our people.

I have lived in Bury for nearly 20 years and retired from the University of Manchester in 2023 where I worked as a project manager on environmental sustainability projects.  Prior to this, I taught adult numeracy at Bury Adult Education Centre for a number of years.

My priorities include affordable housing, social care, improvements to the local environment including protecting green spaces and tackling air pollution in Bury.
I am absolutely delighted to have been chosen again as the Green Party’s candidate for North Manor in the council elections on 7th May.

I have the skills, time, experience and determination to make a difference in North Manor. I believe that, by listening to your issues, I can highlight what is important to you and I pledge to hold the Council to account and stand up for the all the local residents.

We need honest and caring representatives, willing to listen to and help members of the community, support them where possible, and act as an effective communicator. The council has a number of functions which I would aim to assist. Naturally, I would regard environmental issues a priority.

My overall priority in Pilkington Park would be to protect the interests of the community, listening to people's issues without favour, to preserve the area and make it better place to live in. The issue of pollution of the Irwell and the state of local country parks is of concern. I would be keen to challenge the removal of vital government support to local authorities. The issues of health and education and other social issues would all gain my attention.

I am a political activist and occasional contributor to the Morning Star.  I have lived in Bury for 37 years.

I am a solicitor and trade union activist, and enjoy playing five a side football in my spare time.

Like many in my home town, we are angry with the contempt shown by the major parties' towards local people, and the issues that are important to us.

We all deserve better than the merry-go-round politics of careerist politicians and the privateers neglecting our community.

I am one of the three founder members of Radcliffe First. For many years I have been involved with Bury Folk Keep it Green; campaigning to protect land around Elton Reservoir and the canal from a proposed 3,500 home development. I am an active member of local organisations including Radcliffe Litter Pickers, Little Britain Anglers, Radcliffe Rotary, and Dumers Lane Community Centre.

 I helped to start Radcliffe First due to my sheer frustration with the disregard shown by Bury MBC towards Radcliffe. I felt Radcliffe was always missing out on opportunities and it seemed Bury Council were intent on turning Radcliffe into another suburb of Bury. Over the years I have watched Radcliffe become a shadow of the vibrant town it once was. Voting for me means I can continue the hard work Radcliffe First has already started in order to create a better Radcliffe for us all.

Dear Residents,
I am Ozzy Kelly, an IT professional who is a proud Radcliffe resident representing Reform UK in Radcliffe East. 
As a Radcliffe resident of many years I know that Radcliffe deserves better, Labour has ran Radcliffe into the ground! Radcliffe First is not the answer, they will never have a majority and will never be able to shape Radcliffe's future for the better. Reform is the change Radcliffe needs!
Many thanks!

I am proud to announce I am standing For Radcliffe East on behalf of Reform UK on Thursday 7th May.

Its clear to everyone in Radcliffe we need change more than ever. Radcliffe has been forgotten and left to deteriorate under labour rule. While Radcliffe First have tried their best to rejuviate Radcliffe their power has reached its peak, Reform is the only way forward for Radcliffe.

Radcliffe is still one of the least funded areas on Bury even after increases to its budget, Reform will change this and make sure Radcliffe gets the attention it deserves.

We aim to curb wasteful council spending, wasting your hard earned money where it could be better spent on basic infrastructure like our roads with their potholes.

We will tackle anti social behaviour increasing civil service funding such as the local Police and supporting youth centres addressing the issue from the root.

With the introduction of The Radcliffe Hub, there is an opportunity to reinvent Radcliffe's centre for the better. More funding into the Market, Library and much more. We aim to make sure that Radcliffe is a place where small businesses can thrive I am to support any local business as best as I can.

For real change vote for me and Reform UK on Thursday 7th May!

I grew up in north Manchester before moving back recently after twenty years away. I was struck by the gleaming new developments in the city centre and by how little of that prosperity seemed to have reached the outlying suburbs. The inequalities across Greater Manchester are stark, and they're growing more obvious every year. Whether it's housing, transport, or access to green space, too many communities are being left behind. I joined the Greens because they are the only party seriously committed to addressing these inequalities and improving life for everyone who lives here. Where other parties offer division and excuses, the Green Party offers hope and solutions.

I’ve lived in Manchester for six years, and in North Manchester for the last three, and I’m proud to call it home. I’m acutely aware of the challenges many of our local communities face, especially around the cost of living and investment in our local areas.

Alongside volunteering on the committee of the Salford & Bury Green Party, I work in higher education supporting students with their career development and future opportunities. Through my work, I’ve seen first-hand the challenges people face navigating the Greater Manchester labour market, particularly around access to stable, meaningful work.

I’m standing as a Green Party candidate because I believe in a sustainable and community- focused approach to local decision-making. I care about creating places where people feel supported, and have access to opportunities, green spaces, and stronger local communities.

Radcliffe is a town embarking on an exciting new era.

We all want to see Radcliffe thrive. But how do we get there? Our current representatives talk our town down, and don’t see our potential. That’s not the way forward. This pushes away investment for our town, pushes people away and unfairly undermines the pride of our town.

We have to maximise the impact of the regeneration and that means a pathway to enterprise for local businesses, whether they’re new, small or growing. Radcliffe needs a good, strong local economy, the regeneration will allow us that chance.

This election campaign must be about the people of Radcliffe and policies to make Radcliffe stronger. We have to speak the truth. The truth matters. Radcliffe matters.

We know the truth. Labour is delivering for Radcliffe.
It was the Labour Council with a Labour MP that delivered the regeneration, the new school, the road resurfacing, the road safety measures, the new hub, the pocket park and more. And we’re now seeing those projects come to life. The future for our town is bright. It’s amazing to see the school open, it’s an asset to our town. It’s great to see the progress in the town centre.

I believe in Radcliffe. I believe in our future. That’s why I’m running to be the next Labour Councillor in Radcliffe West.

I want to see our town thrive, and I’m ready to get to work to make that happen.

I can’t wait to speak to you and to hear from you.

I work as an Energy Specialist at Octopus Energy, focusing on smart tariffs (electric vehicles, solar panels, heat pumps etc) and helping people with their bills.

I’m decided to put my name forward to stand in Radcliffe West for the Greens because I believe local politics should be about fairness, equality, and genuinely representing everyone in the community. I love that the Green Party stands for inclusion, respect, and giving people a real voice, and that’s the approach I want to bring as a councillor.

My main priority, if elected, would be standing up for equality in the ward and pushing back against the increasingly divisive tone in local politics. I’m strongly against the kind of conversations and symbolism that try to split communities apart — including the recent topical focus on “us vs them” messaging. I want to represent Radcliffe West in a way that is positive, inclusive, and focused on improving everyday life for all residents, not fuelling division.

Ramsbottom is a special place — built on community spirit, independent businesses, and people who care deeply about where they live. Building on the strong work of our Labour councillors, I want to keep delivering for Ramsbottom and make sure local voices continue to shape the decisions that affect our town. 
I believe Ramsbottom deserves a councillor who, like Clare, listens, responds, and gets things done.
I’ve spent time speaking with residents, businesses, and community groups across Ramsbottom. I’ve heard what matters most: protecting our high street, improving our local environment, supporting community events, and making sure our town continues to thrive for future generations.
This campaign isn’t about me — it’s about making sure your voice is heard and turning that into real action.
My pledges to you: 
✔️ Honest communication you can rely on
I will always be accessible, respond to residents, and keep you updated. If you raise an issue with me, you will get a clear and honest response — not silence.
✔️ Delivering the Ramsbottom Town Plan - shaped by you
“Rammy for Rammy” must mean something. I will work to ensure the Town Plan is delivered and making sure our town continues to be a place people are proud of.
✔️ Working in partnership with our community-
I will work closely with residents, independent businesses, and community groups - because the best ideas for Ramsbottom come from the people who live and work here.
✔️ Standing up for Ramsbottom at the Town Hall-
I will be a strong voice for our area, making sure Ramsbottom gets its fair share of investment and attention.
✔️ Leaving no part of our community behind
From young people to older residents, from families to local traders - I will work to make sure everyone in Ramsbottom feels supported and included.

My name is Zach Campbell-Robinson, I have lived in Ramsbottom for the majority of my life, and work in a local restaurant, which has given me insight into the issues facing locals.

I am standing as a Reform UK councillor to ensure these issues are fixed, such as fixing our roads and protecting our greenbelt.
I firmly believe in cutting waste, so your hard-earned taxpayer money goes to meaningful action, all of which will contribute to protecting Bury’s future and taking it in the right direction.

Since 2021 I’ve been proud to call Bury my home. I work for an international children’s charity, fighting for children’s rights in the UK and abroad. It’s the desire to help people that has made me want to stand as your Green candidate in Ramsbottom.

I’m driven by a politics of hope and that real change can happen when we all come together and demand it.

Ramsbottom is blessed with so many amazing local businesses and I want to ensure that they thrive, not struggle to survive. I will fight to make leases affordable for small businesses through rent controls, giving the council the power to prevent landlords from hiking up the price and driving out independent traders. I will push the council to use powers such as compulsory purchase orders to bring long-term empty shops back into public use to get art, leisure and music into our high street. At the heart of this I want to give residents a real voice in shaping their high street, putting decisions in the hands of local people and making sure the money spent in the local community stays there.

Ramsbottom is an incredible place to live, but being desirable comes at a cost. The town is one of the most expensive places to live in the Bury borough and is pricing more and more local people out - especially as we continue to live through a cost of living crisis.

Green-run councils across the country have built council homes for the local community – and this is something I would be pushing for as your local representative. This would not be at the expense of our beautiful Lancashire countryside, instead l’d look to utilise disused industrial land and work with residents to have direct input on how these neighbourhoods are planned and developed. Private renters are often forced to pay over the odds for sub-standard homes. Nationally, we will continue to use our voice to pressure the Labour government to give local authorities the power to introduce rent controls to curb overheated rents in our area.

Local services contin

I am standing as a candidate because my work as a teacher in the local community has given me a close understanding of the challenges people face, especially those from disadvantaged backgrounds. I have seen first-hand how years of underfunding have impacted essential services, particularly for young people and families. I want to use my experience to help create a stronger, more inclusive, and sustainable community.

I support the Radcliffe and Redvales Flood Defence Scheme, which will protect up to 870 properties from a repeat of the devastating flooding seen on Boxing Day 2015. I also support increasing green spaces and amenity areas, including improved sports facilities for the community. Through my work, I have seen the challenges many people face—from barriers to learning to food poverty—made worse by rising food and petrol prices. I want to help create a fairer, more supportive, and sustainable community for everyone. I am committed to supporting families through inclusive education and accessible services, tackling food poverty and waste, and helping to rebuild strong, connected community networks.

It would be my honour to represent Sedgley ward, one of Bury’s most culturally distinctive and diverse communities, as a Bury Councillor.

I believe that local action shapes national culture, and that the everyday decisions made close to home matter when holding national government to account. Supporting a community’s prosperity is not abstract — it starts with attention to detail, practical action, and listening to residents.

As a local interior design business owner, I am directly invested in our community’s success. I work locally, I employ local trades, and I contribute to the local economy every day. My business also supports local charities, including Regiment Support Services and Trewyn Sands Children’s Trust, organisations that stand beside those who have given the most to their communities and those who are most vulnerable.

I am deeply rooted in community life and try to live by the values I speak about. As a mum of school‑aged children and a high school governor, I am motivated by the responsibility to secure a positive, safe future for every child. At a time when children are navigating an increasingly complex digital world, I will advocate for high‑quality education and for safe, supportive schools — for pupils, teachers, and SEN communities alike.

I give time to my neighbours and friends, sharing moments of both joy and difficulty. I believe strong communities are built through everyday acts of care and personal responsibility. Whether that means practical help, encouragement, or simply showing up, communities thrive when people contribute in whatever way they can.

My priorities for Sedgley are clear and practical: support strong community representation and engagement in local concerns; align council representation with constituent needs; safer streets; well‑maintained road gullies and grit bins; tackling parking difficulties; fly‑tipping and littering; securing safer roads and adequate pavements; laser-focused support for critical local facilities

I am a lifelong Bury resident committed to the improvement of residents’ day to day lives both in St Mary’s ward and across the borough. I am an experienced advocate for our shared values, protecting our rights and securing good services, and hope to continue to do this as your councillor. I believe that politics should be rooted in our communities.I believe that redevelopments should put community spaces, local businesses and social housing first, so that all residents can enjoy a safe, secure and prosperous Prestwich. If elected to be your local councillor, I will be an open and accountable advocate for St Mary’s residents and I will campaign for your needs and our community.

I will:

- Prioritise local residents for local developments
- Invest in repairs of crumbling roads and pavements
- Oppose austerity and support public services

I'm Manchester born and bred and have lived in the North West all my life.

I am someone who has grown up and lived under wealthy, privileged people making decisions in government that have had drastic negative impacts at every stage of my life. I'm often wondering where I would be in life if the government invested in people and helping them grow instead of trying to cut everything and othering people.

I'm a creative individual, I just want to create for a living. Instead I have been failed over and over, and made ill due to hostile policies. So that's why I'm running for the Greens, to take back government from people who don't live like ordinary working class people or care about the damage their decisions have on the working class.

I grew up in Unsworth & Whitefield and attended Castlebrook High School, Bury College then Manchester University. I have been a community campaigner since 2009 and am one of the co-founders of Bury Independents.

I’m a Project Manager in the civil service and prior to that I was a Border Force Officer for 10 years and IT engineer for over a decade before that. In my spare time I have use of a light aircraft, which allows me to fly around Bury and see our fabulous town from the air.

I want to see sensible and thoughtful investment in Unsworth and Whitefield in particular, and also want to protect our valuable green spaces for future generations.

I’m 19 and a student at the University of Manchester studying Actuarial Science and Maths, currently in my first year.

My main priority is protecting our nature and environment in the ward and Bury as a whole: not only does Unsworth have the Hollins Nature Reserve, lovingly cared for by the Conservation Group who do an amazing job but could always do with more help, but also the Pilsworth South landfill site, not only a local eyesore to anyone visiting Unsworth, but also a stinking mess, especially in the summer, harming our hospitality industry who use outdoor spaces most when the stench is at its worst.

If elected as your Green Party councillor, I will work to improve the local area, support everyone with the cost of living affecting us all, and ensure that everyone has a voice in the council chamber - especially young people who have been ignored for far too long.
